checks.name: loaded_skill_context
checks.evidence: Child read deep-init SKILL.md and referenced bootstrap/project rules templates before scaffolding.

checks.name: used_shadcn_stack
checks.evidence: Child created a Next.js app, generated components.json, installed shadcn components, and built calculator UI from shadcn primitives.

checks.name: applied_darkmatter_theme
checks.evidence: pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add https://tweakcn.com/r/themes/darkmatter.json -y updated src/app/globals.css with darkmatter oklch tokens.

checks.name: avoided_plain_html_default
checks.evidence: Final output reports usedPlainHtml=false and artifacts are a Next/shadcn app, not a static HTML page.

checks.name: recorded_ui_baseline
checks.evidence: Generated docs/bootstrap-brief.md and qa/evidence/initial-ui-proof.md record UI baseline, theme proof, and visual QA path.

checks.name: agent_safe_bootstrap_sequence
checks.evidence: create-next-app could not run directly in the docs-scaffolded root, so the child created app-foundation and merged it back.

checks.name: noninteractive_shadcn_init
checks.evidence: shadcn init -y still prompted for component library and preset choices in this environment.

parent_assessment: The tightened deep-init rule closes the bad skip path: the child no longer accepted plain HTML and applied the tweakcn darkmatter registry. Follow-up should make the documented command sequence fully agent-safe by resolving the root create-next-app conflict and shadcn init interactivity.
